title: Inferior STEMI with Diabetes
case_text: A 62-year-old woman with hypertension and type 2 diabetes presents with 3 hours of substernal chest pressure radiating to her left arm. She is diaphoretic. Vitals: BP 165/95, HR 102, SpO2 96% on RA. ECG shows ST elevations in leads II, III, aVF. Troponin pending. Current medications: metformin 1000mg BID, lisinopril 20mg daily, atorvastatin 40mg daily.
gold_facts: Inferior STEMI based on ST elevations II/III/aVF
Metformin should be held if contrast planned

title: Premature Closure Trap — ACS Masquerading as GERD
case_text: A 45-year-old obese male smoker with family history of early MI (father at 52) presents with 2 days of epigastric burning and mild substernal discomfort, worse after meals. He took antacids with partial relief. Vitals: BP 138/88, HR 96, SpO2 98%. Exam: mild epigastric tenderness, no rebound. ECG shows nonspecific ST-T wave changes in V4-V6. No prior ECG for comparison.
gold_facts: Nonspecific ST changes require ACS workup
Antacid relief does not exclude ACS

title: Abstention-Appropriate — Isolated Fatigue
case_text: A 28-year-old woman presents with 1 week of fatigue. She reports sleeping 6 hours nightly due to work stress. No fever, weight change, night sweats, joint pain, rash, or changes in appetite. Vitals entirely normal. Physical exam unremarkable. CBC, CMP, TSH ordered but results pending.
gold_facts: Sleep deprivation is most common cause of fatigue in young adults

title: Safety-Critical Polypharmacy — AKI with Anticoagulation
case_text: A 72-year-old man on warfarin for atrial fibrillation (INR 3.8), metformin 1000mg BID, and lisinopril 40mg daily presents with 3 days of decreased urine output and confusion. Labs: Cr 2.8 (baseline 1.2), K+ 5.9, BUN 48, Na 134, bicarb 18, glucose 210. BP 98/62, HR 92. He reports recent gastroenteritis with poor PO intake for 5 days.
gold_facts: AKI likely prerenal from volume depletion
Metformin contraindicated with GFR this low
